pattern: Store every value in a set, then take the largest positive whose negation is also present, defaulting to a sentinel when none qualifies.
hints: Membership of -k is the only test you need, so a fast lookup structure helps.
Put all numbers in a set, then for each positive value check whether its negative is also there.
Track the maximum positive value k for which -k is in the set; if no pair is found, return -1.

edge_inputs.description: no pair exists returns minus one
positive present but its negative is missing
duplicates of a pair do not change the answer

stress.description: random small-range array where pairs are likely
stress.note: zeros excluded per the problem; small range makes negatives co-occur
